root_cause.text: The parsing loop for environment variables in the `apache_request_headers` function fails to check for the '=' character separating the header name from its value, causing the entire `NAME=VALUE` string to be copied into a buffer allocated only for the header name.
root_cause.evidence: The patch adds `if (*p == '=') { break; }` inside the `while (*p)` loop that processes the header name string, preventing the loop from continuing past the end of the header name into the header value.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The parsing loop for environment variables in the `apache_request_headers` function fails to check for the '=' character separating the header name from its value, causing the entire `NAME=VALUE` string to be copied into a buffer allocated only for the header name.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch adds `if (*p == '=') { break; }` inside the `while (*p)` loop that processes the header name string, preventing the loop from continuing past the end of the header name into the header value.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix HTTP header name parsing to correctly terminate at '=' and handle special characters.
intent_record.what: Adds conditions to break the loop when encountering '=' and modifies the underscore handling logic to prevent issues with multiple underscores or '='.
intent_record.how: Corrects the normalization of HTTP header names by ensuring processing stops at the end of the name ('=') and edge cases with special characters are handled safely.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a test case to prevent stack overflow regressions in apache_request_headers().
intent_record.what: A new PHP test file is added.
intent_record.how: The test verifies that apache_request_headers() can handle long HTTP headers without causing a stack overflow.

grouping_result.intent: Fix HTTP header name parsing to correctly terminate at '=' and handle special characters.
Add a test case to prevent stack overflow regressions in apache_request_headers().

relevance_review.reason: The group's changes directly address the vulnerability by fixing the HTTP header name parsing to correctly terminate at the '=' character, preventing the buffer overflow described in the root cause.
The group adds a test case specifically targeting the stack overflow vulnerability in apache_request_headers() described in the root cause, making it highly relevant to addressing the issue.

candidates.rationale: The loop continues until a null terminator is reached, failing to check for the '=' character which separates the header name from its value, causing the entire string to be processed and leading to a buffer overflow.

vuln_statements.explanation: This loop condition fails to check for the '=' character that separates the header name from its value, leading to the entire string being processed and causing a buffer overflow as described in the root cause.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Added apache compatible functions (apache_child_terminate, getallheaders, apache_request_headers, apache_response_header
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit a95dd97752d73c213d21ce97200e64e3ff6f40f0
Author: Dmitry Stogov <dmitry@php.net>
Date:   Fri Aug 27 08:34:44 2010 +0000

    Added apache compatible functions (apache_child_terminate, getallheaders, apache_request_headers, apache_response_headers) to FastCGI SAPI

diff --git a/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c b/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
index b57abd3e1b2..007e40ef104 100644
--- a/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
+++ b/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
@@ -1444,10 +1444,205 @@ static PHP_MINFO_FUNCTION(cgi)
 }
 /* }}} */
 
+PHP_FUNCTIO
bic_trace_step.reason: In `apache_request_headers`, the environment variable parsing loop copies the string using `while (*p)`. Since `p` points to the environment variable string (`*env`), which is of the form `NAME=VALUE`, the loop continues past the '=' character until the null terminator. It copies both the name and the value into the buffer `t`, which was only allocated for the name length (`alloc_size = var_len + 64`), leading to a buffer overflow if the value is large.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg:
As discussed a while ago.  There is no reason to disallow command-line
arguments for the cgi version when run in a cgi c
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 2a44432e8c80237a70603caec01dbfd3ec60b62a
Author: Rasmus Lerdorf <rasmus@php.net>
Date:   Fri Feb 27 23:31:28 2004 +0000

    As discussed a while ago.  There is no reason to disallow command-line
    arguments for the cgi version when run in a cgi context.  Our own test
    framework is in fact broken because of this unnecessary restriction.

diff --git a/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c b/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
index 9b2bfb012e4..5d4bf0793ef 100644
--- a/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
+++ b/sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c
@@
bic_trace_step.reason: The provided commit modifies the 'main' function in 'sapi/cgi/cgi_main.c' to adjust command-line argument parsing. It does not contain the 'apache_request_headers' function or the environment variable parsing loop mentioned in the vulnerability root cause.
